Package org.eventb.internal.ui.eventbeditor

Class EventBTreePartWithButtons

java.lang.Object
org.eclipse.ui.forms.AbstractFormPart
org.eclipse.ui.forms.SectionPart
org.eventb.internal.ui.eventbeditor.EventBPartWithButtons
org.eventb.internal.ui.eventbeditor.EventBTreePartWithButtons
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.eclipse.ui.forms.IFormPart, IStatusChangedListener, IElementChangedListener
Direct Known Subclasses:
AxiomMasterSection, CarrierSetMasterSection, ConstantMasterSection, EventMasterSection, InvariantMasterSection, SyntheticContextViewSection, SyntheticMachineViewSection, VariableMasterSection
public abstract class EventBTreePartWithButtons extends EventBPartWithButtons implements IStatusChangedListener

    • EventBTreePartWithButtons

      public EventBTreePartWithButtons(org.eclipse.ui.forms.IManagedForm managedForm, org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Composite parent, org.eclipse.ui.forms.widgets.FormToolkit toolkit, int style, IEventBEditor<?> editor, String[] buttonLabels, String title, String description)
      Constructor.

      Parameters:
      managedForm - The managed form used to create the Section
      parent - The composite parent of the section
      toolkit - The FormToolkit used to create the Section
      style - The style used to create the Section
      editor - The associated Event-B Editor
      buttonLabels - The labels of the buttons
      title - The title of the Section
      description - The description of the Section

      Parameters:

      toolkit - The Form Toolkit used to create the tree viewer
      parent - The composite parent

    • selectItem

      protected void selectItem(org.eclipse.swt.widgets.TreeItem item, int column)

      item - A tree item
      column - a valid column number

    • statusChanged

      public void statusChanged(IRodinElement element)
      Description copied from interface: IStatusChangedListener
      Called when the status (saved/unsaved) of an element has changed.

      This method is called for each new elements that gets created in the editor, and called again when the edited file is saved to notify that the elements is no longer new.

      Specified by:
      statusChanged in interface IStatusChangedListener
      Parameters:
      element - the element which made the saved/unsaved status change

    • hookContextMenu
